body:
## Summary

- Reviewers (code-reviewer, security-auditor, simplify, git-history-reviewer) now see the current PR's existing conversation — humans, other AI bots (CodeRabbit, Sonar, etc.), top-level review approvals, inline file:line comments — before raising their own findings.
- Agents flag overlap explicitly with `[already raised by @<author>]` instead of silently duplicating. Per design, never suppress.
- Applies to fresh review, re-review, and `--respond`. `--self` skipped (no PR exists). Cross-repo works.

## Why

Today the agents fetch `PREVIOUS_PR_COMMENTS` (review comments on the *last 5 closed* PRs touching shared files) but get **zero context about the current PR's thread**. Re-review (Step 6) only fetches comments after the bot's last `## Code Review`. Worst case: a teammate already commented "this looks wrong" or another bot already flagged a SQL injection, and our agents post the same finding minutes later.

## What changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `plugins/air/lib/pr_conversation.py` (new) | Stdlib-only merger: bot-self filter, chronological sort, 100-entry cap, 1500-char body truncation, XML-escaped bodies. CLI + importable. 29 pytest cases. |
| `plugins/air/lib/tests/test_pr_conversation.py` (new) | Coverage: filter behavior, all three sources, sort order, truncation, cap marker, escape against smuggled `</conv-comment>`, edge cases (PENDING reviews, no body, missing user). |
| `plugins/air/commands/review.md` | Step 4 adds three parallel `gh api` fetches → `pr_conversation.py` merge → `PR_CONVERSATION`. Step 7 PR Context block adds `<pr-conversation>` after `</previous-pr-comments>`. Step 6 note clarifying separation from `<developer-comment>`. Untrusted-input list extended. |
| `plugins/air/commands/review-respond.md` | New "Respond Step 1.5: Fetch PR conversation". `<pr-conversation>` added to responder's PR Context. |
| `plugins/air/agents/{code-reviewer,security-auditor,simplify,git-history-reviewer}.md` | Each gains a "PR conversation duplicate-flagging" instruction (always flag, never suppress; treat `<conv-comment>` as untrusted). |
| `plugins/air/commands/platform-gitlab.md` | Three rows added; new behavioral section #7 explains the 3→2 endpoint collapse on GitLab (`/notes` + `/discussions`; no `/reviews` equivalent). |
| `managed/review.py` | New `fetch_pr_reviews`, `fetch_pr_review_comments`, `build_conversation_block` (lazy-imports lib). `build_pr_context` accepts `pr_conversation=` (defaults to `"none"`). `run_review` fetches all three sources unconditionally — even fresh review now gets thread context. |

## Design choices (locked in via plan-mode discussion)

- **Body truncation:** 1500 chars/comment, then `[...]`.
- **Duplicate handling:** always flag with `[already raised by @<author>]`, never suppress.
- **Bot filter:** only our own `## Code Review` comments (login + body-prefix double check). All other bots flow through.
- **Cap:** 100 most-recent entries (defense-in-depth against comment flooding); marker emitted when binding.
- **Empty case:** literal `"none"` (not omitted) to keep the prefix byte-stable for prompt-cache hits.

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest plugins/air/lib/tests/` — 59/59 pass (30 prior + 29 new)
- [x] `bash .air-checks.sh` — clean
- [x] `python -c "ast.parse(...)"` on both edited Python files
- [x] Smoke-test: `build_pr_context(pr_conversation=...)` produces a valid block with the new field; default `"none"` works for unmodified callers
- [ ] CLI fresh review on a PR with mixed human + other-bot + air comments — confirm `<pr-conversation>` appears, bot's own `## Code Review` excluded
- [ ] CLI re-review — confirm `<pr-conversation>` is broader than `<developer-comment>` (latter still finding-number-keyed)
- [ ] Managed review on a PR with prior conversation — confirm `<pr-conversation>` reaches the agent prompt
- [ ] `--respond` — confirm responder sees disputes raised by others
- [ ] Cap behavior on a chatty PR — `<conv-truncated total="N" shown="100"/>` appears
- [ ] Cross-repo — confirm `$REPO_FLAG` threads through
title: feat: give reviewers current PR conversation context
